actionUnits.condition: Enter trigger: Simulate the Player (referenced by MonoBehaviour_0 'player' = fileID 7425022) moving into the Elevator (1) BoxCollider (id 265568267, m_IsTrigger=1) to invoke AttachToElevator.OnTriggerEnter. Scene MonoBehaviour id 265568265 uses script guid 077cb2d6a53b18f4ea7d76e72138fc77 (AttachToElevator) with the exact serialized field mapping. Note: Current code checks 'if (gameObject == player)' instead of 'if (other.gameObject == player)', so no parenting is expected to occur even when the player enters.

Exit trigger: Simulate the Player (fileID 7425022) leaving the Elevator (1) BoxCollider (id 265568267) to invoke AttachToElevator.OnTriggerExit. Scene MonoBehaviour id 265568265 (script guid 077cb2d6a53b18f4ea7d76e72138fc77) is the exact component instance attached to Elevator (1). Note: Due to 'if (gameObject == player)' the unparenting will also not execute; expected no change in hierarchy.

Enter Elevator trigger volume to invoke OnTriggerEnter on AttachToElevator. Scene MonoBehaviour (id=1067963293) with m_Script guid=077cb2d6a53b18f4ea7d76e72138fc77 matches the provided script AttachToElevator, serialized field player=fileID 7425022. Elevator has BoxCollider id=1067963289 with m_IsTrigger=1 and no Rigidbody; therefore, a different collider with a Rigidbody must move into this trigger. Note: The script checks if (gameObject == player); code inside will execute only if player reference equals this Elevator GameObject (prefab instance fileID=15099978). Otherwise only the Unity callback is hit and no SetParent occurs.

Exit Elevator trigger volume to invoke OnTriggerExit on AttachToElevator. Same MonoBehaviour (id=1067963293, guid=077cb2d6a53b18f4ea7d76e72138fc77). Requires the same Rigidbody-equipped collider to leave the BoxCollider (id=1067963289). The if (gameObject == player) condition must be true to execute player.transform.parent = null; otherwise only the Unity callback occurs.

MonoBehaviour (WinLose) same as above. Trigger loselevel() to set LoseUI active.
MonoBehaviour (WinLose) same as above. Trigger Restart() to cover the public method (currently empty).

Enter platform2 (2)'s trigger BoxCollider (id 606197088, isTrigger=1) with an actor that has a Rigidbody (e.g., the serialized 'player' reference fileID 7425022 if it has a collider+rigidbody). Note: current code checks 'if (gameObject == player)' instead of 'if (other.gameObject == player)'; parenting will only occur if the serialized 'player' actually references this same platform GameObject.

Exit platform2 (2)'s trigger BoxCollider (id 606197088) with the same Rigidbody actor to invoke AttachToElevator.OnTriggerExit on MonoBehaviour id 606197086. Expected behavior per code: if (gameObject == player) then detach (player.transform.parent = null); due to the same comparison, detachment only occurs if 'player' equals this platform GameObject.

Simulate reaching the win condition by invoking WinLose.Winlevel() referenced by Scoremanager.winlosescript.
